channel.title: CrossWire
channel.description: Twice-daily Hermes Agent ecosystem intelligence — releases, community builds, trending issues, articles, and tutorials.

item.title: CrossWire: Hermes — OpenRouter Goes Policy-Aware, Desktop Gets Webhooks + Cron Blueprints
item.description:
CrossWire: Hermes — Release Radar & Ecosystem

Thursday, July 23, 2026 · Morning Brief

OpenRouter Model Picker Goes Policy-Aware (#68679)

PR #68679 from wwpinner is the biggest Hermes development this cycle for MOCR. The OpenRouter model picker currently uses the public catalog and a curated snapshot — meaning it can surface models blocked by your API key's privacy/provider/guardrail policy, or hide models that actually work.

This PR fixes both ends: the authenticated account catalog becomes the source of truth, and direct selections validate against the account's ZDR (zero-data-retention) and policy controls. For MOCR, which routes through OpenRouter for most model calls, this means:

  • No more "model not allowed" failures mid-task
  • The picker only shows models your key can actually use
  • Policy enforcement happens at selection time, not execution time

Still open — worth watching as it progresses through review.

Desktop App: Webhooks Page + Cron Blueprints

Two major GUI additions landed yesterday from austinpickett:

  • #69687: Webhooks page — Full CRUD for webhook subscriptions in the desktop GUI. Parity with the dashboard and CLI. Lists subscriptions, creates/toggles/deletes them, all through the same /api/webhooks endpoints.
  • #70066: Cron Blueprints — Manage cron jobs from the desktop GUI instead of editing config files. Likely pairs with the blueprint system for pre-configured scheduling templates.

MOCR relevance: Both reduce friction for managing MOCR's infrastructure from the desktop. The webhooks page especially matters if you're building integrations that need programmatic triggers — though MOCR's current cron-based dispatch doesn't need it yet.

Matrix Gets a Two-Level Model Picker (#70070)

PR #70070 from MrB0req adds a provider → model drill-down picker for the Matrix bridge. Important for the Matrix surface's model selection UX.

WeCom Security Patch: Fail-Closed Media Resolution (#70069)

PR #70069 from egilewski constrains WeCom outbound local media to files strictly below the cwd or $HERMES_HOME. Relative sources resolve inside the same fail-closed block. Tagged with sweeper:risk-security-boundary — the kind of hardening that says the team is tightening the security model broadly.

v0.19.0 "Quicksilver" Still Current

No patch since the July 20 launch. The ~80% faster cold starts, smart approvals, delivery-obligation ledger, and subagent live transcripts are all in play. You're already on it — no action needed.

New Community Repos This Cycle

  • design-judge-skills (⭐275) — Evidence-driven Hermes skills for design award research, evaluation, matching, and submission prep. Gaining traction steadily — up 40 stars since yesterday.
  • frakio-work (⭐50) — Multi-agent workbench stitching Codex + Hermes + Arc. Described as a "super-stitched workstation" built on multi-agent collaboration.
  • hermes-improvements (⭐8) — Vector memory, adaptive soul, and workflow classification for Hermes. Architectural pattern directly relevant to MOCR's memory layer thinking.
  • agent-console (⭐3) — Lightweight browser-based mission control: chat, sessions, cron, skills, voice, read-only missions. Alternative to the desktop app that's worth watching.
  • slashstack-skills (⭐3) — 11 MIT-licensed skills for coding agents: /preflight, /audit, /ship, /remember. Plain-markdown agent guardrails for any LLM tool stack.
  • loremaster (⭐2) — AI scrum-master / PM skill pack for Hermes: living project wiki (called a "lore vault"), human-approval gates, conflict detection. Project management for agent workflows — directly adjacent to MOCR's operating model.
  • fruvisi (⭐2) — Visual org-chart dashboard plugin for Hermes: areas & groups, team lineups, per-agent OpenRouter model routing.

Gaining Popularity (Month-over-Month)

  • hermes-browser-extension (⭐1,094) — Browser-native side panel for Hermes. Still the fastest-growing community project, now past 1,000 stars.
  • Hermex (⭐904) — Native iPhone app for Hermes. Three weeks old, approaching 1,000 stars.
  • recon-skills (⭐793) — 162 offensive security skills for recon and pentest. Field-validated from 600+ targets.
  • EvoForge (⭐101) — Desktop agent workbench with evolutionary capabilities.
  • memmy-agent (⭐69) — One agent, one memory shared by every AI. Memory-as-infrastructure play.
  • mind (⭐58) — Persistent memory for coding agents: auto-capture, explainable recall, privacy controls, portable storage.

CrossWire: Tech — Apple, Home Automation, Tech News

Thursday, July 23, 2026 · Morning Brief

Foldable iPhone Still Faces Production Hurdles

DigiTimes reports Apple's foldable iPhone project is advancing — Foxconn is running trial production — but the exact launch timing remains undecided due to production challenges. Meanwhile, Samsung previewed a creaseless foldable display that 9to5Mac suggests could be destined for the rumored "iPhone Ultra." Two foldable narratives converging — Apple's supply chain struggles vs. Samsung's display readiness.

macOS 27 Beta Hides Trippy Siri "Voice Pad" With 13 Voices

MacRumors uncovered a hidden "Voice Pad" in macOS 27 Golden Gate beta 4. The interface is a nearly black canvas where your cursor movement causes colors to transition across the view and Siri's voice modulates through different characteristics — spatial audio means voices closer to your cursor sound clearer, while others fade into ambient distance. The pad lays out 13 voice slots spanning gender, age, and character profiles — everything from soft-spoken young female to deep adult male to two older-sounding voices. Activating it requires disabling SIP and debugging — so it's a beta-4 Easter egg, not user-facing yet — but it strongly suggests Apple is preparing a dramatically richer voice selection for Siri AI this fall.

Apple Store App Getting AI Shopping Assistant

Apple updated its Apple Store app privacy policy to mention a forthcoming virtual shopping assistant. The assistant sends chat data to Apple's "partners" (suggesting a third-party AI model), scrubbed of personal identifiers. Apple keeps transcripts for business analytics and, with user opt-in, for improving the assistant. A Siri-integrated LLM for hardware buying decisions — coming to an Apple Store near you.

MacBook Neo 2: What We Know

9to5Mac rounds up everything known about the MacBook Neo 2 following the original model's strong launch earlier this year. No release date yet, but the Neo line is clearly a keeper for Apple's laptop lineup.

12th-Generation iPad: 2027 Update, Not Major

Apple's next budget iPad is coming in 2027 (not 2026). It's not expected to be a major redesign — more of a spec bump for the most affordable tablet. If you're planning a purchase, the wait continues.

Google Account Recovery: Now With Selfie Video

Google added selfie video as a Google Account recovery method, alongside passkeys and recovery contacts. Record a short video of yourself to prove identity — a privacy/security tradeoff that's both convenient and worth thinking twice about.

Microsoft Brings Original Xbox Backward Compatibility to PC

Ars Technica reports the first four original Xbox titles are now playable (and purchasable) on Windows PCs — and they'll run on an 11-year-old graphics card. Microsoft's emulation layer has crossed the console-to-desktop boundary for the first time.

Ars Technica: Orcas, Science Politics, and Tesla's Squeeze

  • Orcas team up to ram sunfish until they explode — "We think this may help younger orcas feed more easily, or it could also just be for fun." Science headlines you can't look away from.
  • White House report: Trump can usher in a "new golden age" of science — Ars calls it "a near-random mixture of real problems and grievances."
  • Tesla Q2 2026: profitable, but barely — Revenue up, but AI spending is squeezing margins.

CrossWire: Commanders — Training Camp Tomorrow

Thursday, July 23, 2026 · Morning Brief

Rookies Report TOMORROW — Football Is Back

The Washington Commanders rookies report July 24 — that's tomorrow. Veterans report July 28. The 2025 season starts now.

11 open practices confirmed between August 1 and August 19 in Ashburn, VA. All sessions start at 8:30 AM ET with gates at 7:30 AM. Free tickets available at Commanders.com/TrainingCamp — max 6 tickets + 1 parking pass per day.

NFC East Training Camp Preview

NFL.com's division preview highlights Washington's headline offseason moves:

  • Odafe Oweh — Signed to a 4-year, $100M deal. The pass rusher Washington has needed.
  • Amik Robertson (nickel), Nick Cross (safety), K'Lavon Chaisson (edge), Leo Chenal (LB), Tim Settle (DT) — Five more veteran starters added to the defensive overhaul.
  • The message from the front office is clear: the 2025 defense was the priority problem, and they've thrown resources at every level of it.

John Riggins' No. 44 Retirement: Week 9

The Hall of Fame running back's number will be retired at halftime of the Week 9 game vs. the Los Angeles Rams (November 8, home). Long overdue for the Super Bowl XVII MVP.

2026 Schedule

17 games remaining. The gauntlet to start: Sep 13 @ Eagles, Sep 20 @ Cowboys, Sep 27 vs Seahawks. Two division road games in the first three weeks. Primetime: Oct 20 (Mon) @ 49ers.

  • Sep 13 — @ Philadelphia Eagles
  • Sep 20 — @ Dallas Cowboys
  • Sep 27 — vs Seattle Seahawks
  • Oct 4 — vs Indianapolis Colts
  • Oct 11 — vs New York Giants
  • Oct 20 (Mon) — @ San Francisco 49ers
  • Nov 2 — vs Philadelphia Eagles
  • Nov 8 — vs Los Angeles Rams (Riggins' No. 44 retirement)
  • Nov 13 — @ New York Giants
  • Nov 24 — vs Cincinnati Bengals
  • Nov 29 — @ Arizona Cardinals
  • Dec 6 — @ Tennessee Titans
  • Dec 13 — vs Houston Texans
  • Dec 20 — vs Atlanta Falcons
  • Dec 27 — @ Minnesota Vikings
  • Jan 3 — @ Jacksonville Jaguars
  • Jan 10 — vs Dallas Cowboys

NFL Headlines

  • Tyreek Hill: Still "no power" in leg — His recovery from a dislocated knee and multiple ligament tears continues. Posts workout video saying his left leg still lacks power.
  • ESPN: 32-team training camp previews — Full projections and storylines for every team as camps open this week.
  • Dallas Cowboys camp preview — They open July 29 in Oxnard. NFC East opponents getting prepped.
